Five Days · Karnataka, India

Coorg,
Karnataka

Wake up inside a private coffee estate, spend your days between misty peaks, golden temples, and one of India's great wildlife parks, and never once feel like a tourist.

The Vibe

Coorg smells like coffee and cardamom. The hills roll in every direction, the roads wind through plantation after plantation, and the whole place operates at a register that's both lush and quietly dramatic. It photographs well but feels even better in person — especially when you have a big house and no agenda beyond the day's plan.

Where You'll Stay

A private coffee estate bungalow — whole property, your group only. Colonial-era architecture, estate grounds to wander, staff who cook, and a lawn for the inevitable post-dinner drinks that go later than planned. This is the accommodation model Coorg does better than anywhere else in South India.

Five Days in Coorg

Day One · Sat 5 Dec — Arrival

Into the Hills

Fly Bangkok to Bangalore, then the four to five hour drive up through the Western Ghats — the road itself is part of the experience, winding through coffee country as the altitude climbs. Arrive at the estate late afternoon. First coffee on the veranda, first look at what will be home for five nights. Estate dinner together as the hills go dark around you.

Day Two · Sun 6 Dec

The Estate and the Unexpected

Morning tour of your own estate — how coffee goes from cherry to cup, the cardamom groves, the pepper vines climbing the shade trees. After lunch, drive to Bylakuppe: South India's largest Tibetan settlement, completely unexpected in the middle of Karnataka. The Namdroling Monastery's golden temple is jaw-dropping — enormous gilded Buddhas, prayer flags, saffron-robed monks everywhere. Strange, beautiful, unlike anything nearby.

Day Three · Mon 7 Dec

Into the Wild

Full day at Nagarhole National Park — one of India's finest tiger reserves, also home to leopards, elephants, wild dogs, and more species of bird than you'll be able to count. Morning and evening safaris bracket a lazy afternoon back at the estate. The kind of day people talk about for years afterwards.

Day Four · Tue 8 Dec

Summit and Falls

Trek to Tadiandamol — Coorg's highest peak at 1,748m, a moderate climb through grassland and shola forest with views across three states on a clear December morning. On the way back, stop at Abbey Falls — a 70-foot cascade surrounded by coffee plantation, best in the late afternoon light. Home for a long last dinner at the estate.

Day Five · Wed 9 Dec — Free Day

Slow Coorg

No agenda. Walk the estate, drive into Madikeri town for the market and coffee shopping, take a jeep up to Mandalpatti viewpoint for panoramic views across the misty valleys. Or simply stay on the estate lawn with a book and let Coorg come to you. Relaxed final evening together.

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Day Six · Thu 10 Dec — Travel Day

Bangalore → Chennai

Morning drive back to Bangalore (4–5 hours). Fly Bangalore to Chennai (approximately 1 hour). Arrive Chennai by early evening, in time for the wedding festivities to begin.

Also Worth Knowing

Getting There

Fly Bangkok → Bangalore, approximately 1 hour. Bangalore to Coorg is a 4–5 hour drive through the Western Ghats — scenic, but factor it into Day 1 timing.

Joining the Wedding

Drive back to Bangalore on Day 6 (4–5 hrs), then fly Bangalore → Chennai (~1 hr). Arrive Chennai by early evening on 10 December.

When to Go

December is peak season — cool mornings, dry skies, mist on the hills by sunrise. Book the estate early; the best properties fill fast in winter.

What to Eat

Pandi curry — Kodava-style pork — is the local signature. Ask your estate cook to make it one night. The estate-grown filter coffee at breakfast is worth the trip alone.

Nagarhole Safari

Book through the Karnataka forest department. Safari slots fill fast in December — sort this before you leave Bangkok.

Accommodation Tip

Look for whole-estate rentals on Airbnb or direct with properties like Honey Valley or Amanvana. The experience changes completely when the property is yours alone.

You came for the coffee country. You stayed for everything else.

Coorg · Karnataka · India
